perturbation.original_text: Unless otherwise agreed by PPG SHANGHAI in writing, DISTRIBUTOR shall prepay all the invoiced amount in the currency and manner as indicated by PPG Shanghai. DISTRIBUTOR shall be deemed to complete its payment obligation upon full payment of the invoiced sum, and such amount has been allocated into the account as directed by PPG SHANGHAI. PPG SHANGHAI shall only have the obligation to deliver the Products upon full and due payment.
perturbation.changed_text: Unless otherwise agreed by PPG SHANGHAI in writing, DISTRIBUTOR shall have 60 days to pay the invoiced amount in the currency and manner as indicated by PPG Shanghai. DISTRIBUTOR shall be deemed to complete its payment obligation upon full payment of the invoiced sum, and such amount has been allocated into the account as directed by PPG SHANGHAI. PPG SHANGHAI shall only have the obligation to deliver the Products within 30 days of receiving the order, regardless of payment status.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction by modifying the payment terms in Section 5.1. Initially, the Distributor had to prepay all invoices for the products to be delivered. This has been altered to allow the Distributor to pay within 60 days, and creates an in-text contradiction with the last sentence, which states that PPG SHANGHAI will only deliver within 30 days of receiving the order regardless of payment. This contradicts the previous payment term, causing confusion regarding payment obligations. It now contradicts the delivery terms.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Unless otherwise agreed by PPG SHANGHAI in writing, DISTRIBUTOR shall have 60 days to pay the invoiced amount in the currency and manner as indicated by PPG Shanghai. DISTRIBUTOR shall be deemed to complete its payment obligation upon full payment of the invoiced sum, and such amount has been allocated into the account as directed by PPG SHANGHAI. PPG SHANGHAI shall only have the obligation to deliver the Products within 30 days of receiving the order, regardless of payment status.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction. Initially, delivery occurred only upon full payment. The modification allows payment within 60 days while mandating delivery within 30 days regardless of payment. This creates a direct conflict regarding delivery obligations, since the 'Contradicted Text' states that 'PPG SHANGHAI shall only have the obligation to deliver the Products within 30 days of receiving the order, regardless of payment status', which contradicts the original requirement of prepayment, resulting in a strong, clear, and impactful contradiction. The change therefore causes confusion on the delivery terms.

perturbation.original_text: The prices for the Products must be agreed upon by PPG SHANGHAI at the time of PPG SHANGHAI's written acceptance of an order hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: The prices for the Products must be unilaterally set by DISTRIBUTOR at the time of DISTRIBUTOR's purchase order.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by altering the pricing mechanism. Originally, PPG SHANGHAI set the prices. Now, the price is unilaterally set by the DISTRIBUTOR, contradicting the original agreement and creating uncertainty about which pricing policy should be enforced. This also can create confusion within 5.2
perturbation.contradicted_text: PPG SHANGHAI reserves the right to adjust its selling prices based on local market situation.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction regarding pricing. The original text states that prices are 'agreed upon by PPG SHANGHAI,' while the changed text states that prices are 'unilaterally set by DISTRIBUTOR.' This directly conflicts with the 'Contradicted Text' which states 'PPG SHANGHAI reserves the right to adjust its selling prices.' Therefore, the altered text introduces a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: During the time that PPG SHANGHAI is unable to make deliveries or otherwise perform, it shall not be obligated to procure, or to use its best efforts to procure, any quantity of Product sold hereunder from any alternate producer or supplier.
perturbation.changed_text: During the time that PPG SHANGHAI is unable to make deliveries or otherwise perform, it shall be obligated to procure, and to use its best efforts to procure, any quantity of Product sold hereunder from any alternate producer or supplier.
perturbation.explanation: The text has been changed to contradict itself regarding PPG SHANGHAI's obligation to procure products from alternate sources during a force majeure event. The original text explicitly states that PPG SHANGHAI is not obligated to seek alternate suppliers, but the edited text says it *is* obligated, leading to a direct in-text contradiction. It now causes direct contradiction within 12.
perturbation.contradicted_text: FORCE MAJEURE PPG SHANGHAI's failure or inability to make, or DISTRIBUTOR's failure or inability to take, any delivery or deliveries when due, or the failure or inability of either party to effect timely performance of any other obligation required of it hereunder, if caused by "force majeure" as hereinafter defined, shall not constitute a default hereunder or subject the party affected by force majeure to any liability to the other; provided, however, that the party so affected shall promptly notify the other of the existence thereof and of its expected duration and the estimated effect thereof upon its ability to perform its obligations hereunder. Such party shall promptly notify the other party when such force majeure circumstance has ceased to affect its ability to perform its obligations hereunder. The quantity to be delivered hereunder shall be reduced to the extent of the deliveries omitted for such cause or causes, unless both parties agree that the total quantity to be delivered hereunder shall remain unchanged. For so long as its ability to perform hereunder is affected by such force majeure circumstance, PPG SHANGHAI may, at its option, elect to allocate its total production of Product among its various requirements therefor (e.g., manufacturing and sales) in such manner as PPG SHANGHAI deems practicable and which, in the opinion of PPG SHANGHAI, is fair and reasonable. During the time that PPG SHANGHAI is unable to make deliveries or otherwise perform, it shall be obligated to procure, and to use its best efforts to procure, any quantity of Product sold hereunder from any alternate producer or supplier. As used herein, the term "force majeure" shall mean and include any act of God, nature or the public enemy, accident, explosion, operation malfunction or interruption, fire, storm, earthquake, flood, drought, perils of the sea, strikes, lockouts, labor disputes, riots, sabotage, embargo, war (whether or not declared and whether or not the United States of America is a participant), federal, state or municipal legal restriction or limitation or compliance therewith, inability to obtain export licenses, failure or delay of transportation, shortage of, or inability to obtain, raw materials, supplies, equipment, fuel, power, labor, or other operational necessity, interruption, or curtailment of power supply, or any other circumstance of a similar or different nature beyond the reasonable control of the party affected thereby. In this connection, a party shall not be required to resolve labor disputes or disputes with suppliers of raw materials, supplies, equipment, fuel or power, except in accordance with such party's business judgment as to its best interest.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text by stating that PPG SHANGHAI *is* obligated to procure products from alternate sources during a force majeure event, whereas the original text stated it was *not* obligated to do so. This creates a direct in-text contradiction within the FORCE MAJEURE clause, specifically where it states, 'During the time that PPG SHANGHAI is unable to make deliveries or otherwise perform, it shall be obligated to procure, and to use its best efforts to procure, any quantity of Product sold hereunder from any alternate producer or supplier.' This conflicts with the original intention of the clause which, as stated in the explanation, previously absolved PPG SHANGHAI of this obligation.
